implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: Ephemeral PostgreSQL + pgvector Docker Compose configuration with dynamic ports
Session-scoped pytest fixture for isolated database instances
Automatic cleanup via finalizers and atexit handlers
Parallel worktree isolation via UUID-based project names
Fast-fail with clear diagnostics when Docker unavailable
Deterministic fake embedding fixture with no external dependencies
Comprehensive integration and unit test coverage (9 tests)

completion_promises.criterion_text: deploy/local/docker-compose.yml exists: image pgvector/pgvector:pg16, host port env-overridable (no fixed 5432 binding; fixture assigns a free random port), mounts ./initdb to /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d, defines a pg_isready healthcheck with interval: 2s, retries: 10
completion_promises.evidence: File exists at deploy/local/docker-compose.yml. Verified contents: uses pgvector/pgvector:pg16 image, PGPORT env var with ${PGPORT:-5432} for dynamic port (no fixed binding), volumes mount ./initdb to /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d, healthcheck with pg_isready, interval 2s, retries 10, timeout 5s, start_period 5s

completion_promises.criterion_text: deploy/local/initdb/01_extensions.sql contains CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;
completion_promises.evidence: File exists at deploy/local/initdb/01_extensions.sql. Verified contents: contains exactly 'CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;'

completion_promises.criterion_text: With Docker unavailable, an explicit python -m pytest tests/integration/ -m integration run fails/skips quickly (no hang) with a message naming Docker / the container runtime as the missing prerequisite
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented in tests/integration/conftest.py lines 106-115: Docker availability check via 'docker info' subprocess, pytest.skip() called with message 'Docker is not available. Integration tests require Docker or a compatible container runtime. Install Docker Desktop or configure your container runtime.' No hang, immediate skip

completion_promises.criterion_text: The session-scoped ephemeral_pg fixture yields a plain postgresql:// DSN at 127.0.0.1 on a non-5432 random port; after session teardown docker compose -p <project> ps -q is empty and the anonymous volume is removed (down -v) — verified by a minimal integration self-test
completion_promises.evidence: Fixture implementation in tests/integration/conftest.py lines 95-180. Random port via _get_random_port() (lines 30-40), DSN format 'postgresql://fleet_memory:fleet_memory@127.0.0.1:{port}/fleet_memory' (line 168), teardown via _teardown_compose() calling 'docker compose down -v --remove-orphans' (lines 76-92), registered via request.addfinalizer and atexit.register (lines 174-175). Verified by test_ephemeral_pg_starts_and_provides_dsn (PASSED), test_ephemeral_pg_uses_random_port (PASSED), test_teardown_removes_container_and_volume (PASSED)

completion_promises.criterion_text: After fixture startup, SELECT extname FROM pg_extension includes vector (init script ran on the fresh volume — review risk R3)
completion_promises.evidence: Verified by test_pgvector_extension_available (PASSED). Test connects to ephemeral_pg DSN, queries 'SELECT extname FROM pg_extension WHERE extname = 'vector'', asserts result is not None and equals 'vector'. Test passed, confirming init script executed successfully and extension is installed

completion_promises.criterion_text: Two fixture instantiations with distinct project uids yield DSNs on different ports with mutually invisible data (parallel-isolation self-test)
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented ephemeral_pg_factory fixture (lines 183-273 in tests/integration/conftest.py) that creates independent instances with unique UUIDs. Verified by test_data_isolation_between_projects (PASSED). Test creates table in first instance, spawns second via factory, confirms different ports (extracted from DSN strings), confirms second instance has no visibility to first instance's table via information_schema query. Test passed

completion_promises.criterion_text: tests/conftest.py exports a fake_embed fixture whose fake is defined INLINE (deterministic 768-dim vectors, no import from fleet_memory.embed — that module lands in a later wave); using it alone opens no database or network connection
completion_promises.evidence: File exists at tests/conftest.py. Verified contents: @pytest.fixture fake_embed (line 18) returns Callable[[str], list[float]], implementation is fully inline using hash-seeded linear congruential generator (lines 34-50), generates 768 dimensions, no imports from fleet_memory modules (only stdlib: annotations, typing, pytest). Verified no network/DB usage by unit tests: test_fake_embed_produces_768_dim_vector, test_fake_embed_is_deterministic, test_fake_embed_different_inputs_differ, test_fake_embed_values_in_valid_range - all PASSED in 0.01s with no external dependencies
